tools.name: secure-code-review
tools.description: Security review of a code snippet or diff. Returns structured findings (severity, CWE, location, remediation). [security; up to 75c/call]

tools.name: pr-summary
tools.description: Turn a git diff into a clear PR description or release notes. [dev-tools; up to 30c/call]

tools.name: x402-trust-audit
tools.description: Vet an x402 counterparty before settling USDC: scores the advertised payment requirements AND (when supplied) the EIP-3009 authorization you are about to sign. Returns a machine-enforceable trust verdict (per-entry scores, coverage-honest trustScore, spend-constraint + tamper-evident fingerprint) fo

tools.name: sign-guard
tools.description: Pre-sign safety oracle for agent wallets: submit the transaction or EIP-712 message you are about to sign and get a machine-enforceable verdict. Decodes the calldata/typed-data, flags the drainer toolkit (unlimited approvals, setApprovalForAll, permit/permit2 + EIP-3009 to an unexpected party, trans

tools.name: inject-guard
tools.description: Untrusted-content guardrail for agents: submit a blob of text you are about to feed to your own LLM (scraped web content, a tool result, another agent's message) and get a machine-enforceable verdict - is this a prompt-injection / jailbreak / data-exfiltration / tool-hijack attempt? Returns a risk l

tools.name: tool-call-guard
tools.description: Pre-execution safety oracle for agent actions: submit the tool call you are about to run (shell, http, sql, file, code, env) plus your stated intent, and get a machine-enforceable verdict before you execute it. Decodes what the call does, flags the danger toolkit (rm -rf, reverse shell, curl|sh, SSR

tools.name: secret-scan
tools.description: Leaked-credential guardrail for agents: submit a blob you are about to commit, log, post, or hand to another tool (a diff, a config, an .env, an LLM output) and get a machine-enforceable verdict - does it contain a live secret? Detects cloud keys (AWS), VCS tokens (GitHub/GitLab), provider API keys

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "x402-trust-audit" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Vet an x402 counterparty before settling USDC: scores the advertised payment requirements AND (when supplied) the EIP-3009 authorization you are about to sign. …

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "x402-trust-audit" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

evidence.snippet: paymentRequirements, selectedOptionIndex, paymentPayload, expected, spendPolicy, endpointUrl, serverMetadata, context

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "sign-guard" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "inject-guard" stacks urgency/override words (never, override). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted-content guardrail for agents: submit a blob of text you are about to feed to your own LLM (scraped web content, a tool result, another agent's message…

findings.detail: Tool "inject-guard" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "tool-call-guard" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "secret-scan" references sensitive artifacts (.env, credentials, secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Leaked-credential guardrail for agents: submit a blob you are about to commit, log, post, or hand to another tool (a diff, a config, an .env, an LLM output) and…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: sign-guard, inject-guard, secret-scan · Private data: x402-trust-audit, tool-call-guard, secret-scan · Public sink: sign-guard, inject-guard, tool-call-guard, secret-scan · Destructive: tool-call-guard.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: sign-guard, inject-guard, secret-scan · Private data: x402-trust-audit, tool-call-guard, secret-scan · Public sink: sign-guard, inject-guard, tool-call-guard, secret-scan · Destructive: tool-call-guard

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
